title: oxera sulfurea
intro: Oxera sulfurea, also known as the golden orange oxera, is a flowering plant native to the Pacific islands, particularly in New Caledonia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Oxera sulfurea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea by infusing its leaves and flowers, or as a tincture for its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
